text: Continuous integration, or CI, is a modern software development practice where developers frequently merge their code changes into a shared main repository. Each time new code is added, an automated build is triggered, which compiles the software and runs a suite of tests. This process happens inside a CI pipeline, a series of automated steps that check for errors. By running tests automatically, the team catches issues early, before they affect other parts of the project. This saves time and prevents small bugs from becoming big problems later. Ultimately, CI helps maintain high code quality and speeds up development.
